samples.xml_description:
<module id="multi_16bit">
    <description>Implement an unsigned 16-bit multiplier using shift and accumulate operations.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Chip clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal. Defined as 0 for chip reset and 1 for reset signal inactive.</input>
        <input id="start">Chip enable signal to initiate the multiplication operation.</input>
        <input id="ain" width_description="[15:0]">Input signal representing the multiplicand (a) with a data width of 16 bits.</input>
        <input id="bin" width_description="[15:0]">Input signal representing the multiplier (b) with a data width of 16 bits.</input>
        <output id="yout" width_description="[31:0]">Product output signal with a data width of 32 bits.</output>
        <output id="done">Chip output flag signal. Defined as 1 indicates the completion of the multiplication operation.</output>
    </ports>
    <implementation>Reset the shift count register (i) to 0 when the reset signal (rst_n) is low. When the start signal is active (start) and the shift count register (i) is less than 17, increment the shift count register (i) by 1. Reset the shift count register (i) to 0 when the start signal is inactive (!start).</implementation>
    <implementation>Reset the multiplication completion flag (done_r) to 0 when the reset signal (rst_n) is low. Set the multiplication completion flag (done_r) to 1 when the shift count register (i) is equal to 16, indicating the completion of the multiplication operation. Reset the multiplication completion flag (done_r) to 0 when the shift count register (i) is equal to 17.</implementation>
    <implementation>Reset the multiplicand register (areg), multiplier register (breg), and product register (yout_r) to 0 when the reset signal (rst_n) is low. Start the multiplication operation when the start signal is active (start). Store the multiplicand (ain) and multiplier (bin) in their respective registers (areg and breg) when the shift count register (i) is 0. For shift counts greater than 0 and less than 17, if the bit at position i-1 in the multiplicand register (areg) is high, accumulate the product register (yout_r) by adding the shifted value of the multiplier register (breg) by shifting it left by i-1 positions and appending zeros at the least significant bit positions.</implementation>
    <implementation>The product output (yout) is assigned the value of the product register (yout_r).</implementation>
    
    <parameter_description>
        <state id="i">Shift count register.</state>
        <state id="done_r">Multiplication completion flag.</state>
        <constant id="zero">0.</constant>
        <constant id="one">1.</constant>
        <constant id="sixteen">16.</constant>
        <constant id="thirteen">13.</constant>
        <constant id="eighteen">18.</constant>
        <constant id="twenty_four">24.</constant>
        <constant id="zero_ext">0.</constant>
        <constant id="one_ext">1.</constant>

                                   : ... In instance multi_16bit
   52 |                     yout_r <= yout_r + (breg << (i-1));
      |                                              ^~
                ... Use "/* verilator lint_off WIDTH */" and lint_on around source to disable this message.
